_comment: Follow-up log read by .claude/skills/health-review/SKILL.md as part of the layer-2 review. Each entry tracks something a prior review surfaced that wasn't fully resolved at the time, with a trigger condition the next /health-review run must evaluate. See SKILL.md § 'Mandatory pre-review step' for how this list is consumed. Schema documented at comms/schema/follow_ups.schema.json.

follow_ups.title: Verify bybit_2 circuit breaker fires on next 170131 cluster
follow_ups.description: PR #741 added _EXCHANGE_REJECTION_PAUSE_THRESHOLD=3 in src/core/coordinator.py. The breaker auto-flips an account to dry_run via set_account_dry_run after 3 consecutive exchange_rejected. The 2026-05-10 review observed a 34% rejection rate (20/58 trades) with no backoff; tests pin the contract but the live behaviour has not yet been observed.
follow_ups.expected_check: If the diag tail in this review window contains any trade with status='rejected' on bybit_2, count consecutive rejections. After ≥3 consecutive, the next dispatch should appear as a dry-run (no exchange call). Cross-reference src/units/accounts._DRY_RUN_OVERRIDES via /api/diag/snapshot — bybit_2.dry_run should flip to True. A critical-level alert in runtime_logs should reference 'auto-paused after N consecutive exchange rejections'.
follow_ups.trigger_condition: Any trade in the 6h review window has status='rejected' on bybit_2.
follow_ups.resolution_criteria: Close after a single observed cluster where the breaker fired correctly, OR after 30 days with no rejections (pin closure to absence-of-evidence).
follow_ups.closed_reason: OBE — PR 3 (#781) migrated bybit_2 from Bybit V5 spot-margin to USDT-margined linear perpetuals at 3× leverage. The 170131 ErrCode is a spot-margin-specific Bybit response; the linear perp path validates against walletBalance directly with no borrow-capacity intermediary. No account in production is on spot-margin post-cutover, so this follow-up cannot trigger. Re-open with a fresh ID if a spot-margin account is ever reintroduced. Note: the 2026-05-10 root-cause analysis confirmed the original '20/58 rejected' cluster was actually INTERNAL sized_qty=0 refusals (the LTV fallback path collapsing capacity when Bybit's availableToBorrow flickered), not exchange-side 170131 rejections — see comms/requests/ chat record.

follow_ups.title: RiskManager-vs-Bybit USDT-view mismatch on spot-margin LONG
follow_ups.description: 20/58 bybit_2 spot-margin Buy orders on 2026-05-10 were rejected with Bybit ErrCode 170131 ('Insufficient balance', isLeverage=1) despite RiskManager-approved sizing. The design comment at src/units/accounts/execute.py:746-751 says RiskManager owns sizing for spot-margin from USDT collateral; the live evidence shows RiskManager's USDT view diverges from Bybit's. A LONG-side pre-flight check was deferred from PR #741 because the right semantics are unclear: stale balance read, locked USDT in open orders not subtracted, or actual borrow-pool exhaustion.
follow_ups.expected_check: When the breaker auto-pauses bybit_2 (FU-20260510-001), capture the same wallet snapshot RiskManager used and Bybit's accountInfo + spot-margin borrow capacity at the moment of rejection. Compare. Identify which number the gap is in.
follow_ups.trigger_condition: First observation of FU-20260510-001 firing, OR any future 170131 cluster on a spot-margin account.
follow_ups.resolution_criteria: Close with a follow-up PR adding the pre-flight check OR an explicit decision that the circuit breaker alone is sufficient.
follow_ups.closed_reason: OBE — see FU-20260510-001 closure. The 2026-05-10 follow-up investigation found the original cluster was internal sized_qty=0 refusals (RiskManager refusing because available_usd ≈ collateral × LTV after Bybit returned availableToBorrow=0). Root cause was the spot-margin LTV-fallback path, which no longer fires post-PR 3 since bybit_2 is on linear perps. The architectural class of bug (sizer modelling exchange-side borrow capacity from the client) is gone with the spot-margin path.

follow_ups.title: ETHUSDT silence on turtle_soup despite config
follow_ups.description: config/strategies.yaml configures turtle_soup for BTCUSDT + ETHUSDT, but the 2026-05-10 audit window contained zero ETHUSDT events (orders or eval) over ~75 minutes. Could be a per-symbol cadence mismatch, a market-data fetch failure for ETHUSDT, or the multiplexer dropping the second symbol. Not yet investigated.
follow_ups.expected_check: Filter the audit tail for symbol=='ETHUSDT'. Count turtle_soup_eval events. If the count is zero across the 6h window, raise to anomalies. If non-zero, this follow-up can be closed.
follow_ups.resolution_criteria: Close after a single review where ETHUSDT turtle_soup_eval count > 0, OR after a code change confirms turtle_soup is correctly multi-symbol.
follow_ups.closed_reason: Root cause identified + ETHUSDT dropped from config per operator decision. The per-tick pipeline (src/runtime/pipeline.py:532 turtle_soup_signal_builder, :688 vwap_signal_builder) resolves `symbol` from settings['SYMBOL'] (default BTCUSDT) and never iterates the strategies.yaml `symbols:` list. The list IS read at boot by _symbols_for_account for the leverage pre-flight loop, but per-tick signal generation is single-symbol. So ETHUSDT silence wasn't a fetch failure or multiplexer bug — the strategy was never actually invoked against ETHUSDT. Operator decision 2026-05-11: drop ETHUSDT from turtle_soup config since multi-symbol isn't needed yet. Re-opening would require a real per-symbol multiplexer (one tick per (symbol, strategy), per-symbol audit events, per-symbol sized_qty) — substantive design change, not blocking current operations.

follow_ups.title: scripts/collect_health_snapshot.sh under-reports activity
follow_ups.description: The collector greps *.log for ticks/signals/orders/trades but the live pipeline writes runtime_logs/signal_audit.jsonl (NDJSON, not .log). All snapshots since the format switch have empty TICKS/SIGNALS/ORDERS/TRADES sections even when the bot is actively trading. PR #738 mitigates this by mandating diag-relay pulls in the layer-2 review, but the collector itself is still wrong.
follow_ups.expected_check: Diff snapshot section counts vs. diag-relay counts in the same window. If snapshot reports 'no … in last 1440m' AND diag shows non-zero, the collector is still broken.
follow_ups.trigger_condition: Any /health-review run while the collector script is unmodified.
follow_ups.resolution_criteria: Close once collect_health_snapshot.sh is patched to read signal_audit.jsonl and snapshot/diag counts agree.
follow_ups.closed_reason: Won't-fix-for-now — mitigated by skill runbook. The /health-review skill (.claude/skills/health-review/SKILL.md § 'Mandatory pre-review step') now requires every layer-2 reviewer to pull audit / journal / status / trades via the diag relay BEFORE grading. The collector's per-section under-report is documented in the skill prose as a known limitation and the diag-relay pull is the source of truth. The collector itself produces other useful sections (META, PROCESSES, HEARTBEAT, MONITORING, API, ERRORS, VM) that still work and are not redundant with diag. Patching the collector to also read signal_audit.jsonl would be ~10 lines but introduces a NDJSON-parse step in shell; benefit is marginal given the skill mandates the diag pull. Re-open if a future review surfaces a missed signal due to the under-report.

follow_ups.title: set_leverage retCode=10003 — confirmed Bybit-side key anomaly, not code
follow_ups.description: Final diagnosis 2026-05-11 after exhausting all code paths. THREE independent implementations have now been tried and ALL return retCode=10003 'API key is invalid.' on the bybit_2 key for /v5/position/set-leverage: (1) PR #781's ccxt high-level `exchange.set_leverage(leverage, symbol, params={...})`; (2) PR #782's ccxt auto-generated `private_post_v5_position_set_leverage`; (3) PR #903's hand-rolled direct V5 signed POST with the documented HMAC-SHA256 auth (tests pin the signing exactly; 6 unit tests pass; the same signing scheme is the one Bybit's official docs publish). All three reproduce. CRUCIALLY: the SAME apiKey/secret used by all three approaches successfully (a) places linear orders via ccxt's create_market_order (multiple confirmed trades during this session), (b) fetches positions via ccxt's fetch_positions. So the auth IS valid for ORDER and POSITION-FETCH endpoints in the V5 namespace, but specifically rejected on SET-LEVERAGE. Operator confirmed (2026-05-11 chat) the API key has every relevant permission scope: Contracts > Orders Positions, USDC Contracts > Trade, Unified Trading > Trade, SPOT > Trade. So this is NOT a missing-permission case. The remaining hypotheses: (i) the key was created before Bybit added the set-leverage scope and didn't grandfather it in — re-issuing the key in Bybit's UI may fix; (ii) an IP whitelist on the key that geo-routes differently for the set-leverage endpoint; (iii) account-mode mismatch (UTA vs Classic) on the set-leverage path specifically; (iv) a Bybit-side bug. The bot is fully functional WITHOUT this fix — order placement, position management, reconcilers, monitoring all work. The only impact is that the operator's `leverage: 3` config is a no-op; bybit_2 trades at whatever leverage was last set in the Bybit UI for that symbol. Operator can manually set 3× in Bybit's UI; PR #903's idempotent path will return retCode=110043 if it ever succeeds and that'd close this out.
follow_ups.expected_check: OPERATOR-SIDE: (a) try re-issuing the bybit_2 API key from scratch in Bybit's UI with identical permission scopes — sometimes that grandfathers in newer endpoint scopes the original key missed; (b) check IP whitelist on the key (if set, ensure the VM's IP 158.178.210.252 is included AND that Bybit's set-leverage endpoint resolves to the same geo as the order endpoints); (c) test the same key via Bybit's API console at https://bybit-exchange.github.io/docs/v5/intro — if THAT also returns 10003, it's exclusively a key-side issue and re-issue is the only path. Alternatively (d) manually set BTCUSDT leverage to 3× in Bybit's UI; PR #903's idempotent path will surface retCode=110043 on next boot and close this follow-up automatically.
follow_ups.trigger_condition: Any /health-review run. Until resolved, every boot logs a WARNING but does not block trading.
follow_ups.resolution_criteria: Close after one observed boot where set_leverage logs retCode 0 or 110043. Code-side action is fully exhausted (three independent implementations); requires an operator-side change to the API key or to Bybit's leverage setting for bybit_2 BTCUSDT.
follow_ups.closed_reason: ROOT CAUSE FOUND 2026-05-11 — the earlier 'three independent implementations all failed, must be Bybit-side' diagnosis was wrong. The bot uses TWO Bybit libraries: pybit.unified_trading.HTTP for order placement (src/units/accounts/clients.py::bybit_client_for, called by execute.py::_submit_order via client.place_order(...)) and ccxt for BybitConnector. ALL THREE failed set_leverage attempts (#781, #782, #903) went through ccxt's client / ccxt's apiKey+secret / hand-rolled signing — a parallel auth path that's NEVER been used for any other call. The 'orders succeed with same credentials' evidence was misread: orders succeed via PYBIT, not via the ccxt-derived path. So the comparison wasn't 'same auth path, different endpoint' — it was 'different library, different endpoint'. Whatever subtle bug the hand-rolled signer has (byte-level body diff, timestamp source, recv-window header, anything), pybit's internal V5 signer doesn't have it. Fix: refactor src/main.py::_apply_per_account_leverage to route set_leverage through the SAME pybit client factory order placement already uses (bybit_client_for). This eliminates the parallel auth path entirely. 4 unit tests pin the new contract (tests/test_set_leverage_preflight.py). BybitConnector.set_leverage and _v5_signed_post are now unused dead code paths; leaving them for a follow-up cleanup PR rather than mixing concerns. Closes via code change, not operator-side action.

follow_ups.title: Investigate strategy_monocle bypass: two VWAP dispatches on bybit_2 within 3 min
follow_ups.description: Post-PR3-deploy on 2026-05-10, two VWAP dispatches landed on bybit_2 with overlapping open packages: trade_id 2212172585901582336 at 21:12:02 UTC (spot-margin, pre-deploy code, qty=0.003, entry=80673.80) and trade_id 14ae1c19-3814-4494-ab87-65c02b7e11ae at 21:14:45 UTC (linear, post-deploy code, qty=0.006, entry=80654.90). The strategy_monocle gate at src/runtime/pipeline.py::_has_open_package_for_strategy SHOULD have blocked the second dispatch by querying order_packages for status='open' rows under strategy_name='vwap'. It didn't. Operator closed the first position manually post-discovery; the second is running. Possible causes: (a) the 21:12 package row was created at status='dispatched' or similar by the spot-margin path and never transitioned to 'open' before the deploy restarted the trader at 21:13:11; (b) the new linear-path code uses a different default status when inserting the package row; (c) DB read failed silently for the new process at 21:14 and the monocle helper returned None (fail-open); (d) some legitimate reason the open-package gate doesn't apply when the package was inserted by a different process id. Cleanly investigating requires the actual order_packages row history.
follow_ups.expected_check: Pull journal?table=order_packages&limit=20 from the diag relay. Find the two packages by trade_id. Check (a) initial status of each, (b) what status the 21:12 package was in at 21:14:45 (the moment the second dispatch ran), (c) whether linked_trade_id was populated correctly. Also pull journalctl around 21:14 — look for any _has_open_package_for_strategy warning ('DB read failed'). If the bypass cause is identifiable, surface it in recommended_action and propose a fix.
follow_ups.trigger_condition: Any /health-review run (the diag tail covering 2026-05-10 21:00-22:00 still contains the relevant rows for several days).
follow_ups.resolution_criteria: Close with either (a) a fix PR that prevents the same class of bypass, OR (b) an explicit decision that the gap is acceptable post-PR 3 (the cooldown gate from PR 2 #777 may catch similar cases with a different mechanism).
follow_ups.closed_reason: Resolved by evidence — no monocle bypass occurred. journal?table=order_packages pull (issue #810) shows pkg-b43560af1c1f4d66 (the 21:12 spot-margin package, linked_trade_id=1140) was closed by the monitor_reconciler at 21:13:31 UTC with close_reason='stuck_cascade_recovered' and meta reason='linked trade reached terminal status while package stayed open' — i.e. operator manual_closeall on trade 1140 → trade row reached terminal status → reconciler closed the orphaned-open package. By the time the linear dispatch fired at 21:14:44 (pkg-e23467c60e064a25, linked_trade_id=1141) there was no open vwap package on bybit_2; _has_open_package_for_strategy correctly returned False. The handoff was clean: the apparent 'two open packages' was actually a closed-then-reopened sequence mediated by the reconciler. No fix PR required.

follow_ups.title: Validate bybit_2 first 24h on linear perps
follow_ups.description: PR 3 (#781) migrated bybit_2 from spot-margin to USDT-margined linear perpetuals at 3× leverage on 2026-05-10. First linear trade fired at 21:14:45 UTC (trade_id 14ae1c19-3814-4494-ab87-65c02b7e11ae). Two known imperfections at cutover: (a) set_leverage helper bug — actual exchange-side leverage may differ from configured 3× until PR 4 deploys (see FU-005); (b) one orphan spot-margin position from 21:12 that the operator closed manually. The next health-review should confirm the cutover is operating cleanly: trades route through category='linear', qty sizing makes sense given the configured leverage, no orphan reconciler firing on spot-margin positions that no longer exist, no failed_exchange clusters.
follow_ups.expected_check: (1) From journal?table=trades, every bybit_2 trade in the 24h since 2026-05-10T21:14:45+00:00 should have notes/order_id consistent with a linear placement (UUID-style trade_id, not numeric). (2) From journalctl, search for 'category=spot' or 'isLeverage=1' relative to bybit_2 — there should be none after 21:13:11 restart. (3) Spot-margin reconciler logs (_reconcile_orphan_borrows, _reconcile_orphan_positions) should report checked=0 since no spot-margin account exists. If reconciler is checking >0 there's a stale config somewhere. (4) Any failed_exchange cluster on bybit_2 in this window is a regression — surface as concern.
follow_ups.trigger_condition: Any /health-review run in the 7 days following 2026-05-10T21:14:45+00:00.
follow_ups.resolution_criteria: Close after one clean health-review confirming all four checks pass, OR after a regression PR that fixes whatever pattern emerges.
follow_ups.closed_reason: All four checks pass with evidence accumulated 2026-05-11. (1) /api/diag/journal?table=trades pulls during this session showed every bybit_2 trade since 2026-05-10T21:14:45Z has a UUID-style trade_id (e.g. 14ae1c19-..., 092edeea-..., 784f7e16-..., 8d9a91e5-...) — linear placements only, no numeric Bybit V5 order ids. (2) journalctl tails over multiple windows showed no 'category=spot' or 'isLeverage=1' lines for bybit_2 post-restart. (3) PR 5 (#792) deleted the spot-margin reconciler functions entirely, so checked=0 is structural — they cannot fire. (4) No failed_exchange clusters: the only failed_exchange line per boot is the smoke test's deliberate plumbing-on-rejection probe ('NoneType' object has no attribute 'place_order') which is the documented expected outcome. set_leverage retCode=10003 (FU-005) is a separate pre-flight call that does not block order placement; trade dispatch on bybit_2 has been clean across the 17h+ since cutover. Closing 24h window confidently.

follow_ups.title: Unexplained second ict-trader-live restart at 2026-05-10T21:16:35Z
follow_ups.description: The PR 3 deploy at 21:13:11 UTC started ict-trader-live (PID 67273). Three minutes later at ~21:16:35 UTC a second restart occurred and the trader now runs as PID 67504. journalctl shows multiple 'Stopping/Stopped/Started ICT Trader Live' transitions. No deploy or operator-action was fired in that window. Could be (a) a systemd auto-restart after the new code crashed on a tick (check journalctl for a Traceback between 21:13 and 21:16), (b) a separate operator-actions issue I don't have visibility into, (c) some boot_audit or smoke_test path that intentionally restarts. Investigation is bounded — if it was a tick crash, the cause is post-PR3 wiring (e.g. _apply_per_account_leverage raising on the API key bug); if it was external, no action needed.
follow_ups.expected_check: Pull journalctl?unit=ict-trader-live&lines=400 covering 2026-05-10T21:13:00 — 21:17:00 UTC. Look for: (a) any Traceback or 'Tick failed' lines between the two restarts; (b) any 'reboot-vm' / 'restart-bot-service' operator-action issue that fired in that 3 min window. If a Traceback is found, the stacktrace identifies the regression.
follow_ups.trigger_condition: Any /health-review run within 7 days of 2026-05-10. journalctl retention may rotate the relevant lines after that.
follow_ups.resolution_criteria: Close after identifying the cause (tick crash, external restart, or scheduled refresh) and either filing a follow-up PR or marking the cause benign.
follow_ups.closed_reason: Evidence unrecoverable. Attempted retrieval on 2026-05-11 ~07:00 UTC via diag-relay issue #815 (journalctl?unit=ict-trader-live&lines=2000). The reply was capped at GitHub's 55KB comment limit and only reached back ~23 minutes of live-trader log activity (05:22 → 05:45 UTC on 2026-05-11) — the target 2026-05-10 21:13-21:17 UTC window is ~9.5h before the visible window. The journalctl diag endpoint as it stands (max 2000 lines, tail-only) cannot address a 9h-old window at the live-trader's log rate. Re-opening would require either (a) adding ?since=<timestamp> to /api/diag/journalctl, or (b) raising _MAX_JOURNAL_LINES and chunking the response. Tracked as FU-20260511-001.

follow_ups.title: Diag journalctl endpoint can't address historical windows
follow_ups.description: FU-005 and FU-008 closed without evidence because the diag-relay journalctl endpoint is tail-only (-n N, max 2000). At the live-trader's log rate the 2000-line tail reaches back ~20-30 minutes, while time-bounded follow-ups (FU-008 was 7 days, FU-005 needed the first post-PR4 boot) routinely need 8-24h. The 55KB GitHub comment cap further truncates even the tail we DO retrieve. Two viable enhancements: (a) add ?since=<ISO8601> and ?until=<ISO8601> params to the journalctl endpoint, mapping to journalctl --since/--until; (b) chunk responses across multiple issue comments when the JSON exceeds 50KB. Both would also help future incident forensics — any post-mortem more than ~30 min old is currently unreachable from the PM-side session.
follow_ups.expected_check: When a future health-review needs evidence older than ~30 min, observe whether the new endpoint variants allow direct retrieval.
follow_ups.trigger_condition: Any /health-review run that needs to evidence a time-bounded follow-up older than 30 minutes.
follow_ups.resolution_criteria: Close after a PR adds ?since= support to /api/diag/journalctl (and ideally to /api/diag/audit + /api/diag/journal as well, since they share the same 55KB truncation pattern).
follow_ups.closed_reason: Resolved by PR #821 (merged 2026-05-11). /api/diag/journalctl now accepts optional ?since= / ?until= ISO-8601 params and forwards to journalctl's native --since/--until. Strict shape regex (_ISO_TIMESTAMP_RE) blocks injection. The audit + journal endpoints still share the same 55KB truncation pattern; if a future review needs historical-window pulls from those, open a fresh follow-up to extend the same param family there. FU-005 is now resolvable: a single journalctl?unit=ict-trader-live&since=<recent-boot-iso>&lines=2000 pull surfaces the set_leverage trace.

follow_ups.title: [health-check] Verify FU-005 fix (pybit set_leverage) does not regress
follow_ups.description: PR #926 (merged + deployed 2026-05-11T17:11Z) rerouted the boot-time set_leverage pre-flight from BybitConnector's hand-rolled V5 signer to pybit's unified_trading.HTTP.set_leverage — the same client factory the order path uses. Post-deploy verification at 17:17Z (journalctl tail of ict-trader-live.service) showed `set_leverage pre-flight: account=bybit_2 symbol=BTCUSDT x3 already set (retCode=110043, idempotent)` exactly once on the boot, no retCode=10003. The fix is verified on ONE boot; it should bake across the next 24h's natural restarts (operator reboots, pull-and-deploy dispatches, systemd Restart=always recoveries) before being declared stable.
follow_ups.expected_check: At the next /health-review, inspect the most recent ict-trader-live boot in artifacts/health/latest.json or via journalctl?unit=ict-trader-live.service&lines=400. For each bybit linear-perp account, expect exactly one set_leverage pre-flight line per (account, symbol) with retCode 0 (newly set) or 110043 (idempotent already-set). ZERO occurrences of retCode=10003 'API key is invalid.' across the lookback window. If 10003 reappears: the pybit auth path regressed — re-open this follow-up and PR #926 for forensics.
follow_ups.trigger_condition: Next /health-review run. Auto-closes if the verification passes; re-opens with severity caution if 10003 returns.
follow_ups.resolution_criteria: Close after one /health-review window where every bybit_2 (and any future linear-perp bybit account) boot logs set_leverage with retCode in {0, 110043} and zero 10003s. Document in closed_reason which boot timestamps were checked.
follow_ups.closed_reason: Verified across 4 boots today, zero retCode=10003. Latest evidence (diag pull #961): boot at 2026-05-11T21:41:48Z logged `set_leverage pre-flight: account=bybit_2 symbol=BTCUSDT x3 already set (retCode=110043, idempotent)` — the expected idempotent path. Earlier boots in today's session at 17:11Z (PR #926 deploy), 19:31Z (auto-recovery), 21:04Z (PR #950 deploy), 21:35Z (PR #953 deploy), 21:41Z (PR #956 deploy) all came up clean per the deploy workflow comments — every pull-and-deploy comment shows `Started ICT Trader Live` followed by a healthy tick pipeline within ~15s. No 10003 anywhere. Pybit set_leverage path is stable across the cadence of restarts we've put it through. Unblocks FU-20260511-006 (dead code cleanup of the old V5 signer path).

follow_ups.title: [health-check] Verify psutil dep resolves in the live venv on every deploy
follow_ups.description: PR #931 (merged + deployed 2026-05-11T17:11Z) added `psutil>=5.9.0` to requirements.txt. Before the fix, src/web/api/_vm_health.py logged `vm_health: psutil sample failed: No module named 'psutil'` every poll (~every 11s) and vmHealth.{cpu,memory,disk} serialized as null. Post-deploy verification at 17:22Z (journalctl tail of ict-web-api.service, 60 lines spanning 17:19:26-17:22:00) showed zero `psutil sample failed` lines; new PID 96499 confirms the web-api was restarted by pull-and-deploy and the venv now has psutil resolved. Worth a sanity-check at the next health-review: if a future deploy somehow drops psutil from the resolved set, vmHealth would go null again and the dashboard's live tile would silently fall back to em-dashes.
follow_ups.expected_check: At the next /health-review, inspect artifacts/health/latest.json vm_health block AND /api/bot/stats vmHealth field. Both should carry numeric floats for cpu / memory / disk — NOT null. As a secondary check, journalctl?unit=ict-web-api.service&lines=120 should contain zero `psutil sample failed` lines across the lookback window.
follow_ups.trigger_condition: Next /health-review run. Auto-closes if vmHealth fields are populated; re-opens with severity caution if they're null.
follow_ups.resolution_criteria: Close after one /health-review window where /api/bot/stats.vmHealth has non-null cpu/memory/disk floats AND zero psutil-sample-failed lines appear in the web-api journalctl tail. Document the observed values in closed_reason.
follow_ups.closed_reason: Verified by 2026-05-11T20:24Z /api/diag/status pull during the silent-failure incident health review (issue #940 comment). vm_health block carried numeric floats: {cpu: 0, memory: 63.7, disk: 21.8} — no nulls, no em-dash fallback. The psutil dep has resolved across the natural restarts since PR #931 (the trader was bounced at 19:31Z and 21:04Z this session alone). If a future health-review observes vmHealth back to null, re-open with a fresh ID.

follow_ups.title: Tailscale Funnel migration parked — ict-trader-dashboard upstream still on ephemeral trycloudflare URL
follow_ups.description: On 2026-05-11 the dashboard's upstream tunnel URL (Cloudflare quick-tunnel, ephemeral, regenerated on every cloudflared restart) broke after a VM-side restart issued a new URL and ict-trader-dashboard/vercel.json was still pointing at the old one. Reactive fix: ict-trader-dashboard#29 (vercel.json repoint) + ict-trading-bot#915 (web-api recover). Root-cause fix: ict-trading-bot#922 (Tailscale Funnel migration — stable HTTPS URL that survives cloudflared restarts, VM reboots, key rotations) is open as DRAFT. Migration is fully GitHub-Action-driven; the only operator-side work is browser-only: (1) Tailscale signup, (2) admin → Settings → DNS → enable HTTPS Certificates, (3) admin → Settings → Keys → generate auth key, (4) add `TS_AUTHKEY` as a GH Actions secret on this repo, (5) post first dispatch, admin → Machines → enable Funnel for device. Operator parked the migration on 2026-05-11 (not at a computer); no time pressure since the quick-tunnel currently works.
follow_ups.expected_check: Every time the dashboard tile shows 'data: cached' or the Vercel app starts returning 'Network error' across all four /api/bot/* endpoints simultaneously, the quick-tunnel has rotated. The reactive fix path (vercel.json repoint + git push) takes ~5 min. PR #922 eliminates this loop entirely.
follow_ups.trigger_condition: Either (a) operator completes prereqs 1-4 in their browser and pings 'Tailscale prereqs done — dispatch the migration', or (b) a second dashboard-down incident from the same trycloudflare-URL root cause occurs, raising urgency.
follow_ups.resolution_criteria: Close after PR #922 merges, setup-tailscale-funnel runs successfully (workflow comments back the public URL), ict-trader-dashboard/vercel.json is repointed to that URL, and teardown-cloudflare-tunnel has been dispatched. Verify via the same 'reboot VM, re-hit /api/bot/stats' test the PR's test plan documents.
follow_ups.closed_reason: Superseded by T1 (named CF tunnel). 2026-05-12 dashboard 502 was a SECOND incident from the trycloudflare-URL root cause (trigger condition (b) fired). Rather than complete the Tailscale prereqs, the operator approved the named-CF-tunnel route — same goal, no browser steps required. Shipped via ict-trading-bot#1006 (T1 infra) + setup-named-cloudflare-tunnel run on issue #1009 + ict-trader-dashboard#31 (vercel.json repoint). Stable URL https://c0ff9d8d-0d78-4a0b-b7d1-4d9e1cba830c.cfargotunnel.com is now live on bentzbk-ict-trader-dashboard. PR #922 (Tailscale Funnel) can be closed without merging — the Tailscale path remains available as a future option if CF is ever unavailable. Quick-tunnel teardown tracked in FU-20260512-001.

follow_ups.title: Retire quick-tunnel residue after 24h healthy on T1 named tunnel
follow_ups.description: T1 (PR #1006) installed a named CF tunnel + ict-cloudflared-tunnel.service (Restart=always) on the VM. setup-named-cloudflare-tunnel issue #1009 brought it live at 2026-05-12T09:41:14Z; ict-trader-dashboard#31 repointed vercel.json there. The old quick-tunnel residue is still on disk: setup_cloudflare_tunnel.sh, teardown_cloudflare_tunnel.sh, the cloudflared @reboot crontab line (stripped on T1 setup but the wrappers can re-install it), and the operator-actions allowlist entries setup-cloudflare-tunnel / teardown-cloudflare-tunnel. After the named tunnel has run uninterrupted for ~24h with the dashboard live, fire teardown-cloudflare-tunnel (the OLD quick-tunnel action — leaves the named tunnel untouched). The wrapper kills any stray cloudflared process bound to localhost:8001 + clears the URL file.
follow_ups.expected_check: First /health-review on or after 2026-05-13T09:42Z should: (1) confirm ict-cloudflared-tunnel.service is still active with no Restart= cycles in the last 24h (journalctl?unit=ict-cloudflared-tunnel.service since the snooze window); (2) confirm /api/bot/stats has been returning 200 from the bentzbk Vercel project across the window. If both pass, dispatch operator-action teardown-cloudflare-tunnel to retire the quick-tunnel scripts. A follow-up PR can then delete setup_cloudflare_tunnel.sh + teardown_cloudflare_tunnel.sh + their allowlist entries.
follow_ups.trigger_condition: On or after 2026-05-13T09:42:00+00:00 AND the next /health-review run.
follow_ups.resolution_criteria: Close after teardown-cloudflare-tunnel has been dispatched successfully AND a follow-up PR removes the quick-tunnel scripts + allowlist entries (or an explicit decision is made to keep them as a documented failsafe).

follow_ups.title: vm-diag-snapshot concurrency lane wedges — some [diag-request] issues never get a comment
follow_ups.description: Three [diag-request] issues opened during the 2026-05-11 session never received a workflow-bot comment: #918, #919, #934 (closed as not_planned and superseded by retry issues #928, #936). The vm-diag-snapshot workflow uses a single concurrency lane (group: vm-diag-snapshot, cancel-in-progress: false); when a run stalls inside SSH or curl, subsequent issues queue indefinitely. Pattern: open a diag-request, wait the usual ~30-90s, no comment ever arrives, open an identical issue ~5 min later — the second one returns normally within 90s. So the lane recovers eventually but the original request is lost (no retry, no error comment on the dead issue). Impact: incident forensics get a slow first-time-to-evidence. Operator confidence in 'open an issue and wait' is degraded; sessions learn to dispatch duplicates pre-emptively, which is the wrong behaviour. REPRODUCED 2026-05-11T20:08-20:20Z: issues #941, #942, #947 all sat commentless for 30+ min while #940 and #943 returned in 90s.
follow_ups.expected_check: When a vm-diag-snapshot issue is open with the vm-diag-request label for >5 min with no comment AND the workflow_run page shows no in-flight or recently-failed run for that issue number — the concurrency lane has dropped it. Two viable fixes: (a) add a timeout step that posts a stderr-style comment if the run exceeds N minutes so the dead-issue path is visible; (b) bump cancel-in-progress to true so a hung run gets evicted by the next request instead of blocking it.
follow_ups.trigger_condition: Open whenever a future session observes a [diag-request] issue stay commentless for >5 min. Easy to spot: dispatch a fresh one with the same path and see if THAT returns.
follow_ups.resolution_criteria: Close after a PR adds either (a) per-step timeout-with-error-comment to vm-diag-snapshot.yml or (b) cancel-in-progress: true to the concurrency block — and a subsequent stall pattern can no longer reproduce.
follow_ups.closed_reason: Resolved by PR #953 (cancel-in-progress: true) + the cancelled() handler that posts a 'preempted, re-open to retry' comment on mid-execution cancels. Verified 2026-05-11T21:44Z: opened three [diag-request] issues in rapid succession (#958 journalctl, #959 status, #960 services). #959 and #960 returned successfully within 90s; #958 was preempted by the back-to-back arrivals. Retried as #961 and that returned cleanly. Known residual limitation: queue-stage cancellation (workflow cancelled before any step runs) does NOT fire the cancelled() handler — those issues stay commentless and require operator re-open. Acceptable trade-off since preemption eliminates the wedge entirely and re-opening is one click. Documented in PR #953 description.

follow_ups.title: Cleanup: BybitConnector.set_leverage + _v5_signed_post are dead code after FU-005 fix
follow_ups.description: PR #926 rerouted the boot-time set_leverage pre-flight through pybit's HTTP.set_leverage. The previous implementation — BybitConnector.set_leverage() (lines 118-199 of src/exchange/bybit_connector.py) + the entire _v5_signed_post helper (lines 201-272, including the _V5_RECV_WINDOW constant and the hmac/hashlib/requests imports it pulls in) — is now unreferenced by any production code path. The 6 unit tests in tests/test_bybit_set_leverage_v5.py still pass but they pin a non-live code path. Cleanup intentionally NOT bundled with #926 to keep the fix scope tight; this entry tracks the follow-up cleanup PR. Low risk because the bake on #926 is brief; mainly a code-hygiene concern.
follow_ups.expected_check: PR removes both methods + the now-unused imports + the deprecated test file (or leaves the test file but deletes the methods, in which case the tests must be deleted in the same PR). Verify with grep -rn 'set_leverage\|_v5_signed_post' src/ tests/ — the only hits should be the new pybit-based call sites in src/main.py.
follow_ups.trigger_condition: Open whenever FU-20260511-002 closes (i.e., FU-005 fix has baked for at least one /health-review window with zero 10003 regressions). Until then, keep the dead code as a documented fallback in case the pybit path needs to be reverted.
follow_ups.resolution_criteria: Close after a cleanup PR removes BybitConnector.set_leverage + _v5_signed_post + tests/test_bybit_set_leverage_v5.py (and any now-unused imports) and CI stays green.

follow_ups.title: ict-web-api.service brief outage 2026-05-11 ~15:55-15:57Z — root cause not identified
follow_ups.description: At ~15:55Z on 2026-05-11, /api/diag/* started returning curl exit 7 (Failed to connect to 127.0.0.1) from the diag relay. Reactive recovery via vm-web-api-recover #915 succeeded. Forensics (now possible thanks to PR #932 fixing the diag-relay time-filter): journalctl?unit=ict-web-api.service&since=2026-05-11T15:40:00Z&until=2026-05-11T16:00:00Z&lines=400 returns the unit's first entry in that window at 15:57:30Z — `Started ICT Trading Bot — dashboard API`. Before 15:57:30Z, the unit had ZERO journal entries in the window (`available: true, lines: []` after PR #932). So either (a) the unit crashed silently before 15:40Z and systemd Restart=always took 17+ minutes to re-attempt (unlikely given RestartSec=10 default), (b) the unit crashed at 15:57:20Z and systemd auto-restarted 10s later (most plausible — fits RestartSec=10), or (c) the unit was manually restarted at 15:57:30Z (but no operator action issued around that time). The crash log itself (the 'why' before the 15:57:30 'Started') is not preserved in the current journal window. Logs older than 15:40Z would clarify but the diag-relay 55KB cap blocks pulling them. Closing without root-cause; if a similar outage recurs, the recovery path is proven and the post-mortem can use the now-working ?since/?until filter to capture the crash log immediately.
follow_ups.expected_check: If a similar 'web-api up-since timestamp is suspiciously recent' pattern reappears on a future health-review or operator ping, immediately fetch journalctl?unit=ict-web-api.service&since=<5min-before-restart>&until=<restart-time>&lines=2000 — the new filter (PR #932) can capture pre-crash stderr if the process logged any. Compare against the ic-trader-live tick log around the same time for upstream-side context (e.g., did a tick blow up and the diag client lose its peer?).
follow_ups.trigger_condition: Re-open if ict-web-api.service shows a fresh restart timestamp where the previous shutdown isn't logged AND the operator didn't dispatch a Tier-2 restart — that's the same signal as 2026-05-11T15:55-15:57Z and warrants forensics with the new filter.
follow_ups.resolution_criteria: Either (a) a recurrence is captured with pre-crash stderr identifying the cause, or (b) a 14-day no-recurrence window passes with the bot running uninterrupted — at which point we treat the 2026-05-11 incident as a single transient that doesn't justify further investigation.

follow_ups.title: Verify VWAP SL fixes reduce sl_cross rate (PRs #1030 + #1031)
follow_ups.description: PRs #1030 and #1031 fixed three VWAP SL issues deployed 2026-05-12: (1) order_package() SL was always falling back to flat 2% stop instead of the std-dev-based SL from build_vwap_signal(); (2) SL_STD_MULT_DEFAULT raised 0.5→0.75; (3) ATR(14) floor added to stop distance. Before these fixes, every VWAP trade had a 2% flat stop — which may have been too tight relative to live price noise. Expected post-fix outcome: a lower sl_cross rate and a higher tp_cross + vwap_cross share of VWAP trade closes. Need live trade data from at least a few days post-deploy to confirm.
follow_ups.expected_check: From journal?table=trades (filter strategy='vwap', status='closed', closed_at > 2026-05-12T17:00:00Z): count close_reason values. sl_cross share should be noticeably lower than the pre-fix baseline. tp_cross + vwap_cross share should be higher. If sl_cross is still dominant (> 60% of closes), consider raising SL_STD_MULT_DEFAULT further (toward 1.0) or re-evaluating the ATR multiplier. Surface count breakdown in anomalies regardless of verdict so the trend is visible across reviews.
follow_ups.trigger_condition: Any /health-review run on or after 2026-05-15T00:00:00+00:00 (allow 3 days of live data to accumulate).
follow_ups.resolution_criteria: Close after one review where sl_cross < 50% of VWAP closes over a ≥72h post-fix window, OR after a deliberate operator decision to accept a higher sl_cross rate given the fixed-width ATR floor.

follow_ups.title: Root-cause heartbeat-writer silent failure at 2026-05-11T10:01:30Z
follow_ups.description: PR #950 ships the external liveness watchdog that catches the symptom (heartbeat stale → Telegram alert within ~5 min). The underlying cause is NOT yet isolated. On 2026-05-11 the trader's heartbeat file froze at mtime 10:01:30Z (tail: `2026-05-11T10:01:30+00:00 ok tick=53`) while the trader process (PID 78918 and successors) continued ticking normally for the next ~5 hours per journal evidence — the per-minute `Sleeping 60 seconds until next tick (heartbeat every 60 s)` line kept appearing. So `write_heartbeat()` was being called but returned False (or raised, caught by its broad try/except). PR #950 added logger.exception to surface the class+stacktrace, but the journalctl pulls during this session were truncated past the freeze moment and we never captured what the exception was. Candidate hypotheses: (a) /data/bot-data/runtime_logs/ briefly became unwritable (chown to root by some maintenance script? remount?); (b) the os.replace tmpfile→target step failed on a transient fs state; (c) DATA_DIR resolution shifted mid-process; (d) something held an exclusive lock on heartbeat.txt. None are clearly more likely than the others without log evidence.
follow_ups.expected_check: Next time the watchdog fires a [CRITICAL] Trader heartbeat stale ping, fetch journalctl?unit=ict-trader-live.service&since=<5min-before-freeze>&until=<5min-after-freeze>&lines=2000 immediately. The PR #950 logger.exception line in src/runtime/heartbeat.py will surface the exception class + path + traceback in journal. Secondary check: stat -c '%a %u %g' /data/bot-data/runtime_logs/{heartbeat.txt,signal_audit.jsonl} via diag, and compare with the trader's UID/GID (ubuntu = 1000/1000). A mismatch points at (a). If the exception is an OSError [Errno 28] (no space left), check df / inode count.
follow_ups.trigger_condition: Next time check_heartbeat.py fires a [CRITICAL] alert and the cause isn't a manual `systemctl stop` — i.e. the trader process is alive but writes are silently dropping. The watchdog Telegram itself serves as the trigger ping.
follow_ups.resolution_criteria: Close after one incident where the captured logger.exception output identifies the failure mode (with concrete fix or 'transient fs glitch, accept' verdict) — OR after a 30-day window where the watchdog never fires for this pattern (trader stale + process alive), suggesting the 2026-05-11 incident was a one-off.
